hero.title: Espacio Verde Foundation
hero.subtitle: For twelve years we've been bringing environmental information to anyone willing to listen: neighbours, schools, local councils and anyone curious about the world around them.

about.title: Who we are
about.text1: It all started in 2013 with a group of neighbours from Quart de Poblet worried about the state of the local orchards and the nearby river. What began as informal meetings in a borrowed room eventually became a foundation dedicated to gathering and sharing environmental information carefully and without rushing.
about.text2: Today we still hold the same idea: we don't sell anything, and we don't ask for donations on every page. We simply make available the data, reports and resources we put together year after year.

areas.title: Information areas
areas.intro: We organise our work around four main areas, each with its own documentation and regular updates.

biodiversidad.title: Biodiversity
biodiversidad.description: Species records of local flora and fauna, annual surveys and monitoring of the ecosystems closest to the Valencian orchards.

climatico.title: Climate change
climatico.description: Temperature, rainfall and drought data for the region, explained so that anyone can follow them, without unnecessary jargon.

educacion.title: Environmental education
educacion.description: Classroom-ready materials: worksheets, games and guides regularly used by the schools we work with.

voluntariado.title: Volunteering
voluntariado.description: Everything you need to know to help out from time to time: tree planting, riverbank clean-ups and data collection days.

recursos.title: Available resources
recursos.intro: Part of our work is writing down what we learn, so it isn't lost and anyone can look it up whenever they need to.

guias.title: Educational guides
guias.description: Short, plain-language documents on specific topics: how to identify an injured bird, what to do during a heatwave in the orchard, and more.

informes.title: Annual reports
informes.description: A summary of the data collected throughout the year, with simple charts and comparisons against previous years.

charlas.title: Talks and events
charlas.description: A calendar of public events, usually held at schools or the local library, on current environmental topics.

q1.question: Can I use your reports for a school project?
q1.answer: Of course. All the information we publish is freely available, we just ask that you credit the source when you use it.

q2.question: Do I need to be a member to receive the guides?
q2.answer: No, there's no membership or fee of any kind. Anyone can write to us and we'll send the material they need.

q3.question: How often do you update the data?
q3.answer: Annual reports are published every January, although some species records are revised as soon as we detect relevant changes.

contact.title: Contact information
contact.intro: If you have any questions about our resources, or simply want to know more, feel free to write to us.
contact.schedule: Monday to Friday, 9:00 am to 2:00 pm
